financial.note: LH holds 24.98% equity as of March 2026; provides operational synergy and capital support, though QH operates independently in the mid-market segment.
SCB acts as a lead financier for QH's debentures (A- rating); rising interest rates increase the refinancing cost of the ~15bn THB liability base.
Holds ~0.73% equity; reflects long-term domestic institutional stability and yield-seeking alignment.

supply_chain.note: Primary supplier of cement and construction materials; QH's 2026 margins are sensitive to domestic building material cost inflation.
QH holds 19.87% of HMPRO; dividend income from HMPRO is a critical non-operating profit driver, often contributing ~50% of QH's net profit.

competitors.note: Direct competition in the urban townhouse and mid-range condominium segments.
Rivalry in horizontal housing projects (detached houses) across Bangkok outskirts and provincial hubs.
Competition for lifestyle-oriented urban residents; SIRI's aggressive 2026 marketing pressures QH's premium positioning.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: As of April 2026, lower rates support mortgage affordability but the 1.4x Net D/E ratio makes debt servicing sensitive to any hawkish shifts.
Directly impacts the presales of townhouses and mid-tier condos; 2026 demand is fragile due to high household debt.
New government regulations on transfer fees and property appraisal values directly impact QH's inventory liquidation speed.
High debt levels (over 90% in 2026) lead to elevated bank rejection rates for QH's mortgage applicants.
